name: Pastel Pretties
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
2. Beat butter until soft. Cream in sugar.
3. Add egg, vanilla, and anise, mixing well.
4. Add flour mixture and mix until incorporated.
5. Chill dough slightly (about 30 minutes).
6. Divide dough into two portions. Return one portion to the refrigerator.
7. Divide the remaining dough into three parts. Color one part pale pink, one part pale green, and one part pale yellow.
8. Roll each colored dough into two 10-inch long ropes. Place the six ropes on a baking sheet and refrigerate.
9. Remove the plain dough from the refrigerator and divide it in half. Return one portion to the refrigerator.
10. Roll the remaining plain dough into a rectangle measuring 10 x 6 inches.
11. Arrange one pink, one green, and one yellow rope through the center of the rectangle, with two ropes on the bottom and the third centered over them. Wrap the plain dough around the ropes to form a "casing."
12. Wrap the roll in plastic and refrigerate for at least 45 minutes.
13. Repeat the process with the remaining colored ropes and plain dough.
14. Slice chilled logs into 1/16-inch slices (approximately 3mm).
15. Place on ungreased baking sheets and bake until firm but not brown, about 15 minutes. Slight browning on the bottom is acceptable.
16. Cool on racks. Store separately from other cookies due to the anise flavoring.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat," and avoid words like "destroy," "burn," "splutter," or "execute."
